producer: pyfemm@0.1.3 + FEMM 4.2 (build 21Apr2019, x64) via Wine 9.0 on ubu-2

execution.wine_arch_deps: wine 9.0 (amd64) + wine32:i386 multiarch (FEMM is 32-bit PE32 Intel 80386)
execution.stderr_note: wine emitted benign 'Read access denied for device Z:' warnings — Z: is the mounted /tmp drive cross-link, harmless for /tmp-rooted solve.

tape_proxy.material: HTS_proxy (mu_r=1, sigma=50 MS/m, no current source)

oracle.source: HTS Modelling Workgroup shared models — htsmodelling.com
oracle.benchmark_referenced: Benchmark #2 (single REBCO tape under transverse applied AC field)
oracle.expected_note: HTS Modelling shared models assume an applied source (transport current and/or external Bext ~ 100-200 mT). Their published B-field at tape centre is order 1e-1 T.
oracle.relative_error_note: UNDEFINED — measured |B| is exactly 0 because the toy proxy has no current source and no applied external field. The substrate runs FEMM end-to-end (artifacts prove it) but the geometry as currently written is unexcited. Rel-err vs oracle is therefore not a meaningful comparison number; the honest reading is 'solver loop closed, but excitation absent — record provisional, see scope_caveats'.

scope_caveats: (g3-a) Toy axisymmetric tape proxy — NOT a real HTS coil. The geometry is a single 5 mm × 2 mm rectangular HTS material block at r=10 mm; there is no current source, no transport current, no applied external field. The closed-form expectation is |B|=0 by Maxwell (no source ⇒ no field), and FEMM correctly returned 0.

(g3-b) FEMM is 2-D axisymmetric — NOT 3-D coupled EM-thermal-quench. AC loss, end-region screening, J_c(B,T,θ) anisotropy, quench dynamics, and mechanical strain coupling are all absent. The HTS Modelling Workgroup benchmarks require at minimum H-formulation 3-D coupling (per arxiv:2311.09177 / FiQuS reference).

(g3-c) hexa-native em_2d kernel absent — per D80 this record is provisional=true. Substrate is a Python adapter calling FEMM via Wine, not a hexa-lang-resident kernel. Kernel-promotion gated on 2nd EM consumer (D72 N+M rule per ABSORPTION.md kernels/em row).

Solver loop is honest-closed: pyfemm 0.1.3 imported, FEMM 4.2 binary launched under Wine 9.0 + Xvfb, .fem written, .ans solved, mo_getb() returned (0,0). The 'install-gated' branch is now NO LONGER blocking on this host. Re-runs with a proper excitation (mi_modifycircprop / mi_addcircprop applied to a coil) would produce nonzero |B| against a textbook closed-form (e.g. finite solenoid Wheeler formula).
Tiny HTS-tape-proxy axisymmetric demo — NOT a calibrated coil. Real rtsc analyze needs a sourced HTS tape J_c(B,T,θ) field dependence + measured V-I tape characteristic to claim absorption.
citations: arxiv:0811.2883 — Pecher / Sirois et al., 3-D FEM HTS magnetization, single-time-step iteration (the GetDP HTS algorithm paper).
arxiv:2311.09177 — FiQuS / GetDP HTS reference (3-D coupled EM-thermal, cited in ABSORPTION.md L307).
FEMM 4.2 — David Meeker, femm.info (Aladdin Free Public License, 21Apr2019 x64 build).
pyfemm 0.1.3 — Python binding, Linux/Mac port by Melina Merkel (June 2021).
HTS Modelling Workgroup shared model files — htsmodelling.com (benchmark suite).
NEXT_SESSIONS.md H-6 (Track E partial) — this run lands the pyfemm-on-Linux-pool path that unblocks the rtsc+analyze cell.

governance.D61: substrate SSOT stays at ~/core/hexa-lang/stdlib/rtsc/pyfemm_magnetics.py; demiurge unchanged.
governance.D72: classify FIRST — FEMM ecosystem, thin adapter in rtsc/; kernel promotion gated on 2nd consumer.
governance.g3: scope_caveats lists (a) toy axisymmetric tape proxy, (b) 2-D not 3-D coupled, (c) hexa-native port absent.
